name: Las Tablas de Damiel — circular through the Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Damiel
name_original: Las Tablas de Damiel — circular por el Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Damiel
region_intro: Hiking in Tablas De Daimiel offers exploration of a unique wetland ecosystem in the semi-arid La Mancha region. The terrain is predominantly flat, characterized by extensive reed marshes, sedge swamps, and riparian forests along the Guadiana and Gigüela rivers. Wooden walkways and boardwalks provide accessible trails across the wetlands, allowing for close observation of the diverse flora and fauna. This national park features a network of paths suitable for various skill levels, with minimal elevation changes. Best hiking trails in Tablas De Daimiel The most popular hiking route is Isla del Pan Trail, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This circular path connects several islands, offering diverse observation points for wildlife and tamarisk forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Tablas de Damiel — circular through the Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Damiel, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. This route provides a comprehensive circular experience through the national park's wetland landscapes. Local hikers also love the Itinerario de la Torre de Prado Ancho (azul) — ida y vuelta por el Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through the wetland, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. This path culminates at a two-story tower offering panoramic views. Hiking in Tablas De Daimiel is defined by its unique wetland ecosystem, extensive wooden boardwalks, and abundant birdlife. The network offers options for easy to moderate ability levels, with many paths accessible for all. The routes in Tablas De Daimiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 250 hikers have used komoot to explore Tablas De Daimiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Tablas De Daimiel?
A: Tablas De Daimiel offers over 10 distinct hiking trails, ranging from easy strolls to moderate loops. The park's unique wetland environment is explored via a network of wooden walkways and boardwalks.

Q: What is the typical terrain and difficulty level of hikes in Tablas De Daimiel?
A: The terrain in Tablas De Daimiel is predominantly flat, characterized by extensive reed marshes and riparian forests. Most trails are easy, with minimal elevation changes, and many are accessible via wooden boardwalks, making them suitable for various skill levels and even wheelchairs.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Tablas De Daimiel?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373932611/isla-del-pan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla del Pan Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 2.9 km long and takes you across several islands, offering diverse observation points for wildlife. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201454493/las-tablas-de-damiel-circular-through-the-parque-nacional-de-las-tablas-de-damiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tablas de Damiel — circular through the Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Damiel&lt;/a&gt;, a 8.4 km route providing a comprehensive experience of the wetland landscapes.

Q: What are the best times of year to visit Tablas De Daimiel for hiking and wildlife viewing?
A: The best times to visit for optimal wildlife viewing and comfortable hiking are generally spring (April to June), autumn, and winter. During these seasons, you'll find a rich diversity of birdlife, both resident and migratory, and more pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Tablas De Daimiel suitable for families with children?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Tablas De Daimiel are family-friendly, being mostly flat and accessible via boardwalks. The short and engaging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373923917/molemocho-mill-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molemocho Mill Trail&lt;/a&gt;, leading to an old mill, is a great option for families. The park's abundant birdlife and observation points also make it an exciting educational experience for children.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the trails?
A: You'll encounter unique wetland ecosystems, including reed marshes, sedge swamps, and tamarisk forests. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5551964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Melocho&lt;/a&gt; (Molemocho Mill) and various strategically placed &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Observation points&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373944935/itinerario-de-la-torre-de-prado-ancho-azul-ida-y-vuelta-por-el-parque-nacional-de-las-tablas-de-daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itinerario de la Torre de Prado Ancho&lt;/a&gt; leads to a two-story tower offering impressive panoramic views of the wetlands.

Q: Is there parking available at Tablas De Daimiel National Park?
A: Yes, parking facilities are available at the entrance to Tablas De Daimiel National Park, making it convenient for visitors arriving by car. Entry to the park is typically free of charge.

Q: Can I reach Tablas De Daimiel using public transport?
A: While direct public transport options to the park entrance might be limited, you can typically reach nearby towns like Daimiel by bus or train. From there, you might need to arrange a taxi or local transport to the park itself. It's advisable to check local transport schedules in advance.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Tablas De Daimiel?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique wetland scenery, the abundance of birdlife, and the well-maintained, accessible boardwalks that make exploring the park a pleasant experience for all.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Tablas De Daimiel?
A: As a National Park, regulations regarding pets, especially dogs, can be strict to protect the delicate ecosystem and wildlife. It is generally recommended to check the official park rules or contact the visitor center directly before bringing your dog, as access might be restricted or require specific conditions.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay near Tablas De Daimiel?
A: While the park itself focuses on natural preservation, you can find various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the nearby towns, such as Daimiel. These towns offer services for visitors looking to dine or stay overnight after their hiking adventures.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Tablas De Daimiel?
A: Given the flat terrain and boardwalks, comfortable walking shoes are sufficient. During warmer months, light clothing, a hat, and sunscreen are recommended. In cooler seasons, layers are advisable. Always bring water, especially as the park is in a semi-arid region, and binoculars for birdwatching.
tips: One of the lagoons closest to what is called the Casas de la Duquesa and that let us see the waters of this lagoon!

Bread Island is one of the best-known hikes in Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park. This circular trail, approximately 2 kilometers long and lasting 1 to 1.5 hours, takes you through several islands connected by wooden walkways. During the hike, you'll pass the islands of La Entradilla, El Descanso, del Pan, de los Tarayes, and del Maturro.

Each island offers different opportunities to observe the park's flora and fauna. For example, on Bread Island, you can see a tamarisk forest where various birds such as the green woodpecker, the hoopoe, and the long-eared owl nest.

The Tablas de Daimiel are a fascinating place to visit!
Free Visit: You can explore the park's trails for free and without prior reservation. The trails are open from half an hour before sunrise until half an hour after sunset.
Guided Tour: There are several guided tour options available to book:
Guided Hiking: Ideal for exploring the park on foot with an interpreter.
4x4 Tour: Explore restricted areas of the park by all-terrain vehicle.
